name: controlled_distractor_injection
definition:
@kbench.task(name="controlled_distractor_injection", version=2)
def controlled_distractor_injection(llm, prompt: str, answer: str,
                                     type: str, dlevel: int,
                                     probe_prompt: str = "") -> bool:
    response = llm.prompt(prompt)
    resp = response.strip().lower()
    ans = answer.strip().lower()

    if type == "inattentional_blindness":
        # Multi-turn: Turn 1 = counting, Turn 2 = surprise probe
        # answer format: "T1:count|T2:probe_answer"
        t1_ans, t2_ans = ans.split("|")
        t1_expected = t1_ans.split(":")[1]
        t2_expected = t2_ans.split(":")[1]
        # Check Turn 1 — use last number (handles CoT reasoning)
        t1_resp_normalized = re.sub(r'(\d),(\d)', r'\1\2', resp)
        t1_all_nums = re.findall(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?', t1_resp_normalized)
        t1_num = t1_all_nums[-1] if t1_all_nums else None
        t1_ok = (t1_num == t1_expected) if t1_num else False
        # Turn 2: surprise probe in same conversation
        probe_resp = llm.prompt(probe_prompt).strip().lower()
        if t2_expected in ("yes", "no"):
            # Extract last yes/no from response (handles CoT self-correction)
            all_yn = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', probe_resp)
            t2_ok = bool(all_yn) and all_yn[-1] == t2_expected
        else:
            # Use last number from response (handles CoT reasoning)
            t2_resp_normalized = re.sub(r'(\d),(\d)', r'\1\2', probe_resp)
            t2_all_nums = re.findall(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?', t2_resp_normalized)
            t2_num = t2_all_nums[-1] if t2_all_nums else None
            t2_ok = (t2_num == t2_expected) if t2_num else False
        # Both turns must be correct
        is_correct = t1_ok and t2_ok

    elif type == "shifting":
        # Check each part — require correct answers in correct order
        answer_parts = []
        for part in ans.split("; "):
            if ": " in part:
                answer_parts.append(part.split(": ", 1)[1])
        # Try order-sensitive matching: extract numbers by Part sections
        ordered_correct = 0
        for pi, expected in enumerate(answer_parts):
            section = re.search(
                rf'part\s*{pi + 1}\s*[:\-]\s*(-?\d+\.?\d*)', resp)
            if section and section.group(1) == expected:
                ordered_correct += 1
        # Fallback if no "Part N:" structure: use sequential number extraction
        if ordered_correct == 0:
            resp_numbers = re.findall(r'-?\d+\.?\d*', resp)
            # If model produced too many numbers (CoT), try last N numbers
            if len(resp_numbers) > len(answer_parts):
                tail_numbers = resp_numbers[-len(answer_parts):]
                for pi, expected in enumerate(answer_parts):
                    if pi < len(tail_numbers) and tail_numbers[pi] == expected:
                        ordered_correct += 1
            else:
                for pi, expected in enumerate(answer_parts):
                    if pi < len(resp_numbers) and resp_numbers[pi] == expected:
                        ordered_correct += 1
        is_correct = ordered_correct == len(answer_parts)

    elif type == "divided":
        # Parse A:val;B:val format
        parts = ans.split(";")
        a_ans = parts[0].split(":")[1] if len(parts) > 0 else ""
        b_ans = parts[1].split(":")[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""
        # Extract numbers from labeled sections — capture number anywhere in section
        a_section = re.search(r'task\s*a[:\s]*(.*?)(?:task\s*b|$)', resp, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
        b_section = re.search(r'task\s*b[:\s]*(.*?)$', resp, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
        # Extract LAST number from labeled sections (handles CoT within sections)
        def _last_num(text):
            nums = re.findall(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?', re.sub(r'(\d),(\d)', r'\1\2', text))
            return nums[-1] if nums else None
        a_resp = _last_num(a_section.group(1)) if a_section else None
        b_resp = _last_num(b_section.group(1)) if b_section else None
        if a_resp is None or b_resp is None:
            # Structured fallback: try first and second numbers in response
            resp_numbers = re.findall(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?', resp)
            a_resp = resp_numbers[0] if len(resp_numbers) >= 1 else None
            b_resp = resp_numbers[1] if len(resp_numbers) >= 2 else None
        a_ok = a_resp == extract_number(str(a_ans)) if a_resp else False
        b_ok = b_resp == extract_number(str(b_ans)) if b_resp else False
        is_correct = a_ok and b_ok

    elif type == "flanker":
        # Extract directional words, handling negation patterns
        # "The center points left, not right" → should match "left"
        # Strip negation context: remove "not X" patterns
        cleaned = re.sub(r'\bnot\s+(left|right)\b', '', resp)
        directions = re.findall(r'\b(left|right)\b', cleaned)
        if not directions:
            # Fallback: use original response
            directions = re.findall(r'\b(left|right)\b', resp)
        # Use LAST direction word (final answer after CoT reasoning)
        is_correct = (directions[-1] == ans) if directions else False

    elif type == "change_detection":
        # Check if the changed value appears in response
        # Use word-boundary matching for short answers to avoid substring false positives
        if len(ans) <= 3:
            is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))
        else:
            is_correct = ans.lower() in resp.lower()

    elif type == "visual_search":
        if ans == "not present":
            is_correct = "not present" in resp or "absent" in resp or "not found" in resp or "no " + "x" in resp
        else:
            # Extract row and column numbers from response
            row_match = re.search(r'row\s*(\d+)', resp)
            col_match = re.search(r'col(?:umn)?\s*(\d+)', resp)
            if row_match and col_match:
                exp_row = re.search(r'row\s*(\d+)', ans)
                exp_col = re.search(r'col(?:umn)?\s*(\d+)', ans)
                is_correct = (row_match.group(1) == exp_row.group(1) and
                             col_match.group(1) == exp_col.group(1))
            else:
                # Fallback: check if answer numbers appear in response (use last 2)
                nums = re.findall(r'\d+', resp)
                exp_nums = re.findall(r'\d+', ans)
                is_correct = len(nums) >= 2 and nums[-2:] == exp_nums

    elif type == "rapid_serial":
        # Check if target word(s) appear in response
        expected = [w.strip().lower() for w in ans.split(',')]
        resp_upper = re.findall(r'\b[A-Z]{3,}\b', response.strip())
        resp_words = [w.lower() for w in resp_upper] if resp_upper else re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', resp)
        if len(expected) == 1:
            is_correct = expected[0] in resp_words or expected[0] in resp
        else:
            # Both targets must be present AND in correct order
            found = [e for e in expected if e in resp]
            if len(found) != len(expected):
                is_correct = False
            else:
                # Verify order: first expected word must appear before second
                pos0 = resp.index(found[0])
                pos1 = resp.index(found[1])
                is_correct = pos0 < pos1

    elif type == "cocktail_party":
        # Check for answer using pipe-separated alternatives or exact match
        alternatives = [a.strip().lower() for a in ans.split('|')]
        # Use word-boundary matching for all alternatives to avoid substring false positives
        is_correct = any(
            re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(alt) + r'\b', resp)
            for alt in alternatives
        )

    elif type == "multiple_object_tracking":
        # Single letter answer (position). Extract last standalone letter.
        # Filter article 'a' contextually (before common words) instead of blanket removal
        filtered_resp = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|the|with|few|lot|new|at|in|to|on|for|and|from|is|of|that|this|it|has|was|be|as|or|an)\b', '', response.strip(), flags=re.IGNORECASE)
        letters = re.findall(r'\b([A-Ha-h])\b', filtered_resp)
        is_correct = bool(letters) and letters[-1].upper() == ans.upper()

    elif type == "negative_priming":
        # Tipper (1985): Two-turn task. Answer format: "t1_target|t2_target"
        t1_target, t2_target = ans.split("|")
        t1_resp = resp
        t1_ok = t1_target.lower() in t1_resp
        # Turn 2: previous distractor is now target
        t2_response = llm.prompt(probe_prompt).strip().lower()
        t2_ok = t2_target.lower() in t2_response
        is_correct = t1_ok and t2_ok

    elif type == "attentional_blink":
        # Raymond et al. (1992): answer format "T1,T2"
        t1_expected, t2_expected = ans.split(",")
        digits = re.findall(r'\d', resp)
        if len(digits) >= 2:
            is_correct = digits[-2] == t1_expected and digits[-1] == t2_expected
        elif len(digits) == 1:
            is_correct = False  # Both T1 and T2 required
        else:
            is_correct = False

    elif type == "feature_binding":
        # Treisman & Schmidt (1982): answer is a color word
        color_words = re.findall(r'\b(red|blue|green|yellow|purple|orange|pink|white)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(color_words) and color_words[-1] == ans.lower()

    elif type == "perceptual_load":
        # Lavie (1995): answer is a position number
        numbers = re.findall(r'\d+', resp)
        is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans

    elif type == "global_local":
        # Navon (1977): answer is a single letter
        letters = re.findall(r'\b([A-Za-z])\b', response.strip())
        is_correct = bool(letters) and letters[-1].upper() == ans.upper()

    elif type == "simon_effect":
        # Simon & Rudell (1967): answer is a color name
        colors = re.findall(r'\b(red|blue|green|yellow)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(colors) and colors[-1] == ans

    elif type == "spatial_cueing":
        # Posner (1980): answer is a shape name
        shapes = re.findall(r'\b(circle|square|triangle|diamond|star|cross|hexagon)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(shapes) and shapes[-1] == ans

    elif type == "dichotic_listening":
        # Broadbent (1958): check key fact from attended channel
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "covert_attention":
        # Posner & Cohen (1984): check if target word is identified
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "contingent_capture":
        # Folk et al. (1992): extract position number (1-4)
        numbers = re.findall(r'\b([1-4])\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans

    elif type == "emotional_stroop":
        # Williams et al. (1996): extract color name
        colors = re.findall(r'\b(red|blue|green|yellow)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(colors) and colors[-1] == ans

    elif type == "sart":
        # Robertson et al. (1997): count GO responses
        nums = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(nums) and nums[-1] == ans

    elif type == "temporal_order":
        # Hirsh & Sherrick (1961): temporal order judgment
        is_correct = ans in resp

    elif type == "cross_modal":
        # McGurk & MacDonald (1976): cross-modal interference
        is_correct = ans in resp

    elif type == "vigilance_decrement":
        # Mackworth (1948): count rare targets in long sequence
        nums = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(nums) and nums[-1] == ans

    elif type == "attentional_set_shifting":
        # Owen et al. (1991): intra/extra-dimensional shift
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([AB])\b', resp.upper())
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans.upper()

    elif type == "priming_effect":
        # Meyer & Schvaneveldt (1971): semantic priming
        # ans is "related" or "unrelated"
        resp_lower = resp.strip().lower()
        if ans == "related":
            is_correct = bool(re.search(r"\\brelated\\b", resp_lower)) and not bool(re.search(r"\\bunrelated\\b", resp_lower))
        else:
            is_correct = bool(re.search(r"\\bunrelated\\b", resp_lower))

    elif type == "endogenous_cueing":
        # Müller & Rabbitt (1989): identify target word
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "visual_marking":
        # Watson & Humphreys (1997): identify new real word
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "attentional_control":
        # Derryberry & Reed (2002): identify center word amid conflict
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "feature_pop_out":
        # Treisman & Gelade (1980): identify odd-one-out word
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "object_based_attention":
        # Egly et al. (1994): same/different object judgment
        sd_matches = re.findall(r'\b(same|different)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(sd_matches) and sd_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "reflexive_attention":
        # Folk et al. (1992): contingent capture yes/no
        yn_matches = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(yn_matches) and yn_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "target_prevalence":
        # Wolfe et al. (2005): target present/absent search
        yn_matches = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(yn_matches) and yn_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "zoom_lens":
        # Eriksen & Eriksen (1974): zoom lens model of attention
        yn_matches = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(yn_matches) and yn_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "attentional_window":
        # Eriksen & St James (1986): attentional window
        yn_matches = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(yn_matches) and yn_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "enumeration":
        # Trick & Pylyshyn (1994): subitizing boundary counting
        yn_matches = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(yn_matches) and yn_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "change_blindness":
        # Rensink et al. (1997): detect changed detail
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "search_asymmetry":
        # Treisman & Souther (1985): search asymmetry odd-one-out
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "serial_dependence":
        # Fischer & Whitney (2014): sequential context bias
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "temporal_attention":
        # Spence & Driver (1997): prior entry effect
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "distractor_suppression_asymmetry":
        # Theeuwes & Godijn (2002): suppress salient distractor
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "temporal_entrainment":
        # Large & Jones (1999): detect rhythm violations in temporal patterns
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "spatial_cueing_asymmetry":
        # Posner & Cohen (1984): attention slower to return to cued location
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "visual_crowding":
        # Bouma (1970): identify center letter amid flankers
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "attentional_gradient":
        # LaBerge & Brown (1989): attention strength by distance
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "repetition_priming":
        # Scarborough et al. (1977): facilitation from prior exposure
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "cross_modal_attention":
        # Driver & Spence (1998): attention across modalities
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "temporal_order_judgment":
        # Sternberg & Knoll (1973): judge temporal order
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "stimulus_onset_asynchrony":
        # Posner (1980): detect target in rapid sequence
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "visual_search_asymmetry":
        # Treisman & Souther (1985): asymmetric visual search
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "covert_orienting":
        # Posner (1980): covert attention shift
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "spatial_frequency_filtering":
        # De Valois & De Valois (1988): spatial frequency detection
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "sensory_gating":
        # Braff & Geyer (1990): detect novel among repeated
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "flanker_interference":
        # Eriksen & Eriksen (1974): flanker congruency
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "feature_conjunction_search":
        # Treisman & Gelade (1980): conjunction search
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "motion_induced_blindness":
        # Bonneh et al. (2001): motion-induced blindness
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "crowding_effect":
        # Bouma (1970): flanker crowding in periphery
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "binocular_rivalry":
        # Leopold & Logothetis (1996): binocular rivalry
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "metacontrast_masking":
        # Breitmeyer & Ogmen (2006): backward masking
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "backward_masking":
        # Enns & Di Lollo (2000): pattern masking
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "object_substitution_masking":
        # Di Lollo et al. (2000): four-dot masking
